name: Order of Operations — BODMAS
whatIsIt: When a sum mixes operations, we must all do them in the SAME order or we get different answers. The rule is BODMAS: Brackets first, then Orders (powers/roots), then Division and Multiplication (left to right), then Addition and Subtraction (left to right). So 2 + 3 × 4 = 2 + 12 = 14, not 20.
whyNeeded: Calculators, spreadsheets, and every mathematician agree on this order — without it, the same expression could mean two things. It's essential for algebra, formulas, coding and any real calculation with more than one step.
realLifeUses: Working out a bill: 2 items at ₹50 plus a ₹20 delivery = 2×50 + 20
A formula like area then adding a border
Any recipe or cost that scales one part but not another

vocabulary.meaning: Brackets, Orders, Division/Multiplication, Addition/Subtraction — the order to calculate.
( ) group a part to be done first.

story.title: Two Answers, One Sum
story.text: Isha and Rohan both work out 2 + 3 × 4. Rohan goes left to right and gets 20. Isha multiplies first and gets 14. 'We can't both be right!' says Rohan. Isha explains BODMAS: 'Multiplication comes before addition, so 3 × 4 = 12 first, then + 2 = 14.' Rohan checks on the calculator — it agrees with Isha.
story.extractedProblem: What is 2 + 3 × 4 by the correct rule?

visual.caption: Do 3 × 4 = 12 FIRST, then add 2 → 14. Multiplication before addition (BODMAS).

standardMethod.summary: Scan the expression and do operations in BODMAS order, working left to right within the same level.
steps: B — do anything inside BRACKETS first.
DM — then Division and Multiplication, left to right.
AS — finally Addition and Subtraction, left to right.

commonMistakes.mistake: Just working left to right, so 2 + 3 × 4 becomes 20.
commonMistakes.fix: Do × and ÷ BEFORE + and −. 3 × 4 first = 12, then 2 + 12 = 14.

commonMistakes.mistake: Ignoring brackets and multiplying the wrong part.
commonMistakes.fix: Brackets come FIRST — always finish what's inside ( ) before anything else.

easy.q: In BODMAS, what do you do first?

options.label: He added first — should be 4×2=8, then 6+8=14

challenge.answer: He added first — should be 4×2=8, then 6+8=14

questions.q: Which comes BEFORE addition in BODMAS?

teachBackPrompt: Write a mixed sum like 4 + 6 × 2 for a grown-up and ask them to solve it. Then show the BODMAS order and explain why multiplication happens before addition — check it on a calculator together.

revisionCard.summary: BODMAS: Brackets → Orders (powers/roots) → Division & Multiplication (left to right) → Addition & Subtraction (left to right). So 2 + 3 × 4 = 14 (not 20). Always finish brackets first.
